lines.text: The Vitreum Dancer suddenly lost control... Luckily, no one got hurt.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Empathy…?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Telepathy… Yes, according to legend, the Sentinel speaks directly into the mind of the Laureate. Could that be what happened?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Yes, it was. The Montelli family has been promoting the use of Reserved Terminals and Echoes.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: As far as I know, the Montelli family has been promoting the use of Reserved Terminals and Echoes.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Most Ragunnesi use Common Echoes and don't know much about the Reserved ones. The Montellis spotted a chance and started researching Reserved Terminals.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: They push to bring back the Carnevale so they can showcase their Reserved Terminals, prove the quality of their Echoes, and attract both customers and investors.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Oh sorry, just a minute.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Yes, Mr. Alberto. An Echo was...
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: ...I see.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Sorry to keep you waiting. That was Mr. Alberto from the Montelli family. Also my employer.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: He extends a sincere apology for what just happened. They too are at a loss to explain what caused the accident.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: He told me that the Montelli family would investigate further. Please rest assured.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: No... After all, such an incident only hinders their promotion of Reserved Terminals. They have no reason to rejoice over it.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Come to think of it, that Echo... it was looking at you. It seemed to target you.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Hmph, I won't pry into my ward's privacy. I only remember what I need to get the job done. Anything else I accidentally overhear just slips my mind.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: My orders are to guard and to guide. I do my job. Nothing more, nothing less. So if you want to investigate, I'll stick with you until you're done.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Don't thank me, thank the family who hired me. Their generosity is beyond what you can imagine.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Thank you for trusting in my abilities.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: So, what's our next move?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: It was complete chaos, but I spied the direction it fled… We can try our luck at the Pioneer Association.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: I know someone there whose Forte might help us uncover some details we missed.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Good. Let's go this way. It's right over there.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: No problem.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Madame Nyarla, I saw you. Your mask gave you away.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: But scaring the guests is just your twisted sense of humor, isn't it?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: I hope this didn't bother you. <ano=Ombra Elusiva>Invisibility</ano> is Madame Nyarla's Forte. Madame Nyarla, this is the distinguished guest invited by the Montellis, {PlayerName}. {Male=He;Female=She} is very interested in Carnevale masks.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Ahem, we spotted a fellow on the street with a mask that caught our eye… I didn't expect it to be Mr. Gilberto from the Fisalia family.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: The Fisalias are a mixed lot—while some hold honor, others bring disgrace to the name.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: I see why it's left a deep impression…
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: So, the trail leads to the Fisalia family.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: The Fisalia's residencies are near the Cathedral of Mercury. We could stop by the Order for more information.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Thank you for the lead, Madame Nyarla. Oh, and we will reconsider the mask's design. Thank you for your time.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Legends say the Sentinel quelled the storm and stilled the waves, sparing countless lives at sea.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: To show their gratitude, people place candles around the statue, keeping them lit both day and night. Every boat that passes pays their respects.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: It may seem fanciful, but it's best to show respect as we pass by.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: We are now in the Fisalia Residencies territory. We should... Wait, look over there…
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: He looks like the man we saw in Mr. Fulmine's memory. Isn't that Gilberto?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Let's follow him.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Greetings, Sir Antonio. I'm Zani from the Averardo Bank.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: This {Male=gentleman;Female=lady} here is a distinguished guest of your family, {PlayerName}. {Male=He;Female=She} was attacked by a rogue Vitreum Dancer while dining, and we're investigating.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Take it. In Ragunna, if you want help, you must offer a reward and one only declines a reward when they refuse to help.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: From what we know, the Montelli family has no motive for such incidents. They've been anticipating the Carnevale's arrival.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: But the Fisalia family is another story. They've been against the celebration from the start, perhaps they could be behind everything? But I don't understand, what could they hope to gain by stopping the Carnevale?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Why do I feel like I've gotten myself into something above my pay grade…
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: You... have a hunch, hm?
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: You're right. Let's go.
lines.quest_name: The Sacred Breeze So Often Breathes

lines.text: Strange, did Gilberto just vanish into thin air?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: I see. A <ano=Flying Echo>Wingray</ano>, that explains it.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Yes, it's a type of Echo that can fly. People in Ragunna often use them to travel quickly between locations.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: If Gilberto set up this Wingray as an escape route, it should lead us straight to him.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Let's use the Grapple to hitch a ride on the Wingray.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Greetings, madam. My name is Zani. Averardo Bank.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Well... It's probably because I haven't been to church much... Ahem. Please don't stare at me like that. There are more pressing matters.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: We're investigating the Echo incident from the Carnevale rehearsal at Whisperwind Haven, as requested by the Montelli family. One of our clues led us here.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: May I?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Sorry, we didn't see the Echo you mentioned, but we've found clues on their odd behavior. They all point to a member of the Fisalia family, Gilberto.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: He's also caused similar incidents here recently... Echoes went out of control and attacked each other.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Fine by me. With someone from the Order as a neutral witness, the Fisalias won't be able to accuse us of fabricating evidence.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: As long as I'm not treated like a heretic and get kicked out of Rinascita later.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Something feels off. We should go check it out, {PlayerName}.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Uh-oh, things just went south. Looks like we've got a fight on our hands.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: He just turned Echoes into Tacet Discords. Is it the petals?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: How pathetic. You're nothing but a discarded pawn.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: ...Which is not unusual with the Fisalia family.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: I appreciate your passion for the Order, Miss Phoebe, but now is not the time. We need to uncover the real culprit behind the Fisalias.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: He shows no remorse for his crimes. I'll contact Mr. Alberto and have the family send someone to take over.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: What? That's the... The Nimbus Sanctum.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: They are all mad... So this is what they've been planning all along.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: You two go ahead. I'll stay here and watch him until the family arrives, just in case he gets any bright ideas.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: I agree. But what about you, {PlayerName}...
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: All right. There's a port nearby where you can catch a Gondola. It can take you close to the Atrium of Reflections.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: After we hand Gilberto over to the family, we'll meet up with you as soon as possible.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: That flower... looks like the petals we found in Whisperwind Haven.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Masks of Madness... I recall those people at the entrance of the Atrium mentioned it.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: A rite... You mean the Carnevale?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: What a wild turn this job's taken… Seeing the legendary Lorelei up close? Almost makes it worth the trouble.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Hmm? Are you all right, Phoebe?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Speaking of that Vitreum Dancer… What brought it here? Lorelei's song, or maybe the Sentinel's guidance?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: But those wearing the Masks of Madness... The Fractsidus, right? I'm curious about their role in all this. I think they supplied Gilberto with the flowers to turn Echoes into Tacet Discords.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: What's our next move, {PlayerName}?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: You're right... Those snakes lurked in the shadows, using Gilberto as their fall guy. We can't expose them just by ourselves.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Oh, another thing. Since the Fractsidus is conspiring with the Fisalias, that makes them an enemy of the Montelli family.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: There happens to be an auction at Averardo Vault today. The top members of the family will be there.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: We should tip off the family about the Fractsidus' scheme. The Montellis can't afford to ignore this threat.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: What about you, Phoebe?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Then I believe this is where we part ways.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: I guess "singing" is our only option for clearing that barrier.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: But which song?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Meaning it should be a "chorale"? There are also the inscription in front of the barrier: "We mourn the martyrdom of the Blessed Maiden."
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Any chorales you know ring a bell, Phoebe?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: The Blessed Maiden? We met a playwright named Cristoforo earlier. He shared some stories about her.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Phoebe, if you know this chorale, why not give it a try? It might just clear the barrier.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Everyone is in position. We'll manage the west plaza.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Phoebe is stationed at the east plaza, keeping an eye on things.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: The main stage is all yours for the grand performance.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Yes. But I've never seen them in action before. The "Averardo Bank Employee Manual" says they can easily take out a whole squad of Resonators.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Of course, this doesn't apply to someone powerful like you, {PlayerName}. Still, their combat skills are formidable. Their mere presence is enough to make everyone feel safe.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: This facility houses nearly half of Rinascita's wealth. It serves all of Ragunna's citizens, even including the Fisalias.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Despite our feud, no one in Ragunna can refuse the services of Averardo Vault.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: What the... I thought they only responded to hostility!
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Steady now. Don't move. It's on high alert but won't attack unless provoked. At least, that's what the Employee Manual says!
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Montelli… You are the…
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: It was you who tasked me with receiving {PlayerName}?
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Copy that.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Oh it's fine. Just a bit of extra work. I can't stand by and watch my colleagues getting hurt.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Besides, you have bigger things to worry about. Just let me handle this.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Yeah, yeah… That's the burden of being good at my job.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Averardo Vault is just up ahead. It seems this is where we part ways, Noah.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: I'm sure it can find its way back to the harbor. Don't worry. Now, we just need to follow this path to reach the vault. Let's go.
lines.quest_name: Veils Off in Sun or Shadow

lines.text: Ah, there you are. It's been a while since we last worked together.
I was not expecting you to take this job.
Ah, well worth the trip then.
The family's favor is more precious than money. Remember to use it on something worthwhile.
Now about the job—Lady Carlotta must have briefed you in her letter. I'm curious what the Order is thinking stashing their relic in the Montelli vault.
At any rate, my job here is to keep you safe. Take this pass, it's the only one that allows visitors access to the vault's interior.
Time to go. The Order's rep is waiting for us.
It's been a while. Mind if I ask what you were doing just now?
This train Echo? It's been running since the vault was built. It chugs along, doing its thing in silence.
But I do believe your company has made its day.
But if I remember correctly, you are not the representative the Order informed me of.
Huh. Did the thought of working with the Montellis scare her away?
...I'm glad you see it that way. Now, do you have it?
Thank you.
Same here, but it doesn't matter. Lady Carlotta gave me this job. I just follow orders, no questions asked. That's how you work for the Montellis.
Our top priority is to get the job done, nice and clean.
By the way, {PlayerName}, the device from Lady Carlotta should have top security clearance, so you'll be able to operate the train Echo. I hope you enjoy driving.
The vault's security mode has been activated. Someone touched something they shouldn't have.
Unlikely. All personnel were sent home at the start of this assignment. The highest security protocols are in place too.
We can't, not while the vault's security Echo is there. It's capable of sensing danger.
Once it detects a threat, it locks down the vault, jams all signals, and activates a bunch of troublesome Echo guards.
Ha, a triple whammy.
There is, but it's a bit tricky. Anyway, we need to access the security room first. Your clearance might come in handy.
Don't worry. Keeping you safe is part of my job.
The security control room in this depository is right ahead. You can put your device to good use there.
Each depository has its own security room. Just verify your device here to obtain security clearance. It's only valid for this depository, though.
Necessary for the vault's security, but yes, it's a hassle.
We're stuck here for now, and the only way out is to get clearance from each of the depositories. That means going deeper into the vault.
Beats me. The security room only controls the depository it's in and grants access to the next.
I don't know. Maybe a handful of people who've worked here for over thirty years might.
Let's get clearance first. It might help us avoid most of the defense systems triggered by the lockdown.
These Echo guards are controlled by the security system. You can also command them with your clearance.
The higher your clearance is, the more obedient they become.
The longer the vault stays in lockdown, the more defense systems and Echo guards get activated.
Hmm... I can't understand a word.
So, Ash left this letter to tell his grandson about that mysterious "secret cache." Turns out it really does exist.
Exactly. It's just like Acolyte Phoebe said. He triggered the lockdown to get to the depths of the vault without interference.
If he'd acted like a professional and stuck to the plan instead of messing around, we wouldn't be in this mess.
We need to find him before he puts himself or others in danger. Plus we need the security module to leave. Let's head to the lower control room and check the surveillance cameras.
Since this book belongs to the Montellis, I'll hold onto it for now.
Okay, let's sort out the clues.
The lockdown is still in full effect, triggered when "Ash" removed the security module. To escape, we need to retrieve the module and use our clearance to reach the vault's depth.
On top of that, thanks to him, we're stuck dealing with the defense systems.
So, for both his sake and ours, we need to catch him as soon as possible.
Makes sense. He has no choice but to go to the train Echo's terminus. So...
That would save us a lot of trouble. I can help manage the security systems. With the clearance, I should be able to override some defenses and open shortcuts.
Everything's under control now. The vault schematics show the next stop is the terminus. That man has nowhere to hide.
But I'm not sure where the terminus is exactly. The Employee Manual only states that the Echo Depository is located at the vault's depth, without any further information about the station.
Right. Let's get this done quickly, {PlayerName}.
Thanks to the train Echo, we're still in one piece, though I have no idea where we are now.
We'll need it to get to the terminus.
Any details you can remember would be useful. Since employee training didn't touch on this area, we'll be going in blind in search of the exit.
It seems the vault's structure is more complicated than we thought.
Plus, we need time to find the exit.
Let's get going. We need to retrieve the stolen security module. That's our key to getting out.
Bad news. I know nothing about deciphering passwords.
It's nothing but riddles to me.
Security lockdown… That's odd. The Employee Manual says this mode can only be activated by...
Um, miss. If I may, what are you doing?
Wait, even if it is malfunctioning, you shouldn't hit it with your—
What the?!
No... never mind.
This is only speculation, but what if this "Ash" stole from the vault and took the security module? That would explain why a complete lockdown was triggered.
Pretty much. It's the monitoring core that powers this entire depository. If we don't get it back, we'll be stuck here for good.
A vault employee could easily access the module, but nobody would dare steal it. Everyone knows that'll trigger a lockdown of the entire vault.
Don't tell me they're searching for... Never mind, we need to catch this rat before they cause us more trouble.
Let's go find out.
Lady Carlotta has tasked us with a special delivery. May I see your ID, Ms. Lily?
Thank you. Hmm... Confidential Researcher? The vault does have that position, but a researcher shouldn't be in this section.
The ID is real—the anti-counterfeit features check out.
That thing you're carrying seems to disagree.
Agreed. And if she is somehow involved, we'd save a lot of effort by taking her with us.
I'm sorry, ma'am, but you'll need to come with us.
Good. Time to use your clearance, {PlayerName}. Let's find out what's really going on in this vault.
Ma'am, does the name "Ash" mean anything to you?
So the real Ash did exist. As for that man hiding over there... he might have stolen Ash's ID and gotten stuck here.
If it were me, I could maybe last... Well, ten years tops.
Yeah, well, personally I just hope to live long enough to make the most out of my retirement someday. Anyway, we've got an imposter on our hands posing as "Ash."
Let's take the train to the Echo depository. We must find this thief and retrieve the security module. After that, we can decide what to do next.
{PlayerName}？
Do you know what these two were up to?
They were babbling about "hostages" and "feints," and then they came at me.
And they just ran after taking one look at you, hm? Seems about right.
Thieves, you mean? I can imagine why they were fleeing like that.
I have. But that name… it's already...
Just two thieves. Took care of them. I was about to deliver them to the <te href=800005>Chamber of Discipline</te> myself.
Same as you, drawn here by the sound of a scuffle.
I know the rules. I'll go with you, make the report, and be done with it.
Let me handle this. You'd rather not deal with their questions, hm?
So there was somebody else...
This might be more complicated than it looks. Will you wait for me?
This won't take long.
Strange... my memory shouldn't be failing me, should it?
Well, that was a wasted trip.
Perhaps... but the real question is why.
Is that all the nerve you've got? And you call yourselves the ones to bring back the Black Alley?
How many times have you pulled this off?
Save the rest for the Chamber of Discipline.
{PlayerName}, I'll keep digging into the Black Alley and what happened today.
I'll let you know when I have something.
Well, after handing over those other two thieves, I learned they were targeting Montelli staff residences.
Providing a safe working environment is part of my job description too.
It's getting late. We'll pick this up tomorrow, once I've got more to go on.
{PlayerName}, I've got some updates on the situation.
The name of their leader—Talos.
Mmm... that name should've died along with the Black Alley years ago.
Well... the rumors call them... the "Blazing Nightwalker."
The Blazing Nightwalker. Ugh, don't make me say it again.
If you're free, come by and we'll talk. There's something I could use your help with.
Which one should I pick?
Hmm... I'll take all of them. One of each, please.
Ah, {PlayerName}, you made it. Let's sit over there and talk.
I wasn't sure what you liked. It was too hard to choose, so I just got one of each flavor.
Work is bitter enough as it is. Why drink something bitter on top of that?
Yeah... Still about the stuff from last time.
Actually, a lot of the family's staff have been running into the same kind of trouble as that night.
They're not anything special on their own.
What matters is... a lot of the family's employees have been running into the same kind of trouble as that night.
A vacation is like an ad. Only the ones dishing it out have the power to make it real or fake.
And recently, a lot of the family's employees have been running into the same kind of trouble as that night.
Guess my vacation will just have to be postponed again.
Hmm... Maybe that could work.
So, where should we start?
As I mentioned before, many people working for the Montellis have been victims of attempted thefts, like last night.
The family has tightened security, but just sitting here waiting won't solve anything.
As for the ones we caught... they haven't been chatty. Plus, they've only committed a few small-time crimes.
So it would be tricky to use... more extreme methods on them. If we do, it could give the wrong people a chance to twist the situation.
You mentioned someone asked for your help earlier, right?
We've been trying but haven't found anyone who matches her description yet. She's probably keeping a low profile.
But right now, she might be the only lead we've got.
So, might I ask you play your face card one more time?
Much appreciated.
Maybe some more then?
We'll do it tonight. It will be in your hands.
Feel free to rest until it's time. I've already made arrangements at a hotel.
You know what. I'll be here for a while. If you have any questions, just ask away.
Everything is taken care of.
{PlayerName}? It's not time yet. Aren't you going to rest a little longer?
I am resting, alright?
I'm afraid I can't help you with that.
Insomnia and I go way back.
What about?
If we send too many people, we risk tipping our hand.
Well, the Chamber of Discipline did catch a few perps, but they're not quick to act without solid proof.
And... some of the higher-ups in the Order might just be happy to see the Montellis having a little trouble.
Really? That's the biggest issue you find?
Alright, alright... You've got sharp instincts. Spending too much time with you, and I'm running out of secrets.
I was the one who delivered Talos and those Black Alley leaders to The Order.
Yeah, that's me... the Blazing Nightwalker. But honestly? I really don't want to keep repeating that name.
Oh, you're talking about the stories where, after a lot of hardship and misunderstanding, one side lets the truth out with their last breath, leaving the other to regret it forever?
I've got my own ways to keep entertained.
Hiding my true identity got old fast. Since you were curious, I might as well just tell you.
After all, you're not someone I can't trust, right?
Well, as you have already known, I am the Blazing Nightwalker... though I really wish I didn't have to keep repeating that name.
Honestly, it's a bit of a burden...
Oh, and by the way, after you became the Laureate, they're giving you all sorts of monikers too.
The family sent me to handle this, partly because they didn't want a big operation to stir things up, and partly because... well, it was a strategic move.
I have to say it again, I never called myself that.
Back then, I was just doing what needed to be done. Never planned on picking up a fancy nickname.
Well, that's what work does to you...
...Not sure. Could just be the same old story repeating itself.
Let's call it a cleanup job.
If things go smoothly, maybe we can grab a late-night snack after, hmm?
I don't think this is serious enough to give it your all.
Just do enough to get it done. But keep some of that enthusiasm for the snacks later, hm?
Let's move in. Now!
Hey! Don't be causing trouble for folks who need their breakfast bright and early.
Alright, start talking. Where's your hideout? Who's the one really calling the shots?
Looks like we'll have to drag him back. We'll get answers sooner or later.
A shame this one's only good for squealing. He's not the one we're after.
Before we go any further, shouldn't you explain who you are first?
The entrance to the Black Alley is shut. It's done.
A fish that slipped the net...
Sounds just like how the Black Alley ran things... But do people actually accept her as the acting Talos?
I'll wait outside.
{PlayerName}？
The operation is a wrap. Bring the ones we caught in and then everyone can call it a day.
No, nothing to report.
It's just us now. Anything else bothering you while I'm at it?
So that's it. You are worried the Terminal was supplied by someone from the Montelli family, aren't you?
That's why you doubt us and prefer to put your faith in a wayfaring Laureate, hm?
There's no need to apologize. You're a victim in this, with every right to be suspicious.
So then, {PlayerName}, what do you think we should do?
*Sigh*... Who told everyone to call it a day again?
As an employee of the Montelli family, I have an obligation to stop any Terminals from being misused.
I'll just have to ask the boss for double overtime pay and an extra vacation after this.
Are you really bringing up something so depressing now?
Anyway, that's the real story behind that ridiculous name.
Not everything needs to be turned into some grand tale.
In this city, it's mostly the same routine every day.
How long can I snooze before I'm late...
Which time's best to line up to get a fresh wrap...
How to sleep without lying in bed for hours...
These little routines are fragile. One bad move and they're gone.
But likewise, just one bold move is enough to bring them back.
Most people got it wrong, slapping that big name on me.
It's just part of my routine. Like snoozing, eating, or keeping my hair and horns in check.
I was alone back then. I didn't even bother hiding who I was.
And that's when the trouble started to find me.
I thought, why wait for it to get worse? Better to finish it quickly.
But of course, I made it worse... Too fast, too soon.
The Montelli family reached out to me and offered to clean up the mess. Even said they'd back me up in the future.
So, I changed jobs. The work is the same. No difference.
Disappointed?
No, I'm not letting it bother me.
Cleaning up simple scum doesn't weigh on me.
Just reliving some old memories, that's all.
But you're right about something...
What you said just now applies to you, too.
The difference, though, is that you are a blade. A weapon that can break the cycle.
But you don't seem to realize it. Seeing you has brought some old feelings back to me.
Don't you see? Ever since you helped make the Carnevale happen, Ragunna's been different.
Things are busier now. There's more work, more opportunities.
I told you before that these routines are fragile. One bad move and they're gone, right?
So... a stable environment is the only way to reduce the bad moves. That's the way we get to keep our routines.
I tried to do that for a long time, but you're the one who made it happen.
With fewer bad actors on the streets these days, <ano=The Blazing Nightwalker>I</ano>... am increasingly irrelevant.
Perhaps one day, I'll finally be able to sleep through the night.
Of course. It was all part of the routine.
I've been thinking... It's a relief that someone like you is willing to step up.
Your potential is probably beyond what anyone can imagine...
Yet, you still insist on being responsible for everything, no matter how big or small.
Thank you. You've done well.
Ah, sorry... It's just a habit...
Hmm... Okay.
Alright... that's enough.
Hey, you didn't say you were going to do that...
By the way, this is for you.
It used to be my weapon.
You can take it as a token of silence, or as part of an agreement.
Oh, as if I'd do that! That would just be getting in your way.
{PlayerName}, have you got him?
So we won't need to "help" him talk after all.
Once he coughs up the entrance location, we'll head straight there from here.
The family is almost on site. They can handle the cleanup.
Might be a bit late to ask, but… Colleen, you did what Talos asked, didn't you?
Did you forget what {PlayerName} said? Trust goes both ways.
Then when the time comes, make sure you tell the Chamber of Discipline everything. No lies.
Don't excuse yourself by saying you were forced into it. Can you do that?
Ugh, don't call me that.
Believe me, I'd kill to be in a bed right now instead of standing out here.
Let's get this over with.
I'm praying it doesn't reek in there.
"Demons always live in hell, like the Primus always stays in the Cathedral." Yeah, that's about right.
Oh, and {PlayerName}, masks on.
The mask makes things a lot easier.
It's better if neither of us gets recognized too easily.
Guess I don't need to spell it out for you.
Here. Sorry to keep you waiting.
Mmm... those Chamber of Discipline folk are as difficult to deal with as ever.
Well... you know how they are with the Montellis these days. No surprise they didn't share any interrogation details.
I told them the thieves claimed to be with the Black Alley, but the Chamber doesn't bother with rumors without solid proof.
A gang made up of criminals, although how many joined willingly is anyone's guess.
A lot of vagrants were pressed into their service. Pawns in a game of crime.
The leadership got wiped out a few years ago by someone working solo. The leftovers? They were screwed. Some are still rotting in prison.
Maybe. Or maybe they just wanted to spook you... but they picked the wrong person to mess with.
You're still thinking about that person who asked for help, huh? Got any leads?
You think she's in the same boat as those vagrants in the past? Forced into something they didn't choose?
Ah, {PlayerName}, you're the type who can't turn your back when someone asks for help.
It's not too late. Got time for a detour?
The old entrance to the Black Alley.
If they're crawling back from the ashes, we might find a clue or two at their old haunt.
Looks like we're thinking the same thing.
I'll cause a distraction up front. Leave the show to me.
And you'll move to checkmate.
Got it. You're clear to act when you see fit.
I'll watch your back and keep an eye out for anyone trying to shadow you.
No one's been affected. Yes, no worries. Leave the rest to me.
Ah, it's you. What a coincidence.
A short yet violent quake hit Ragunna not long ago. We still don't know the cause, but we're checking for casualties.
Fortunately, the damage seems minimal.
That explains the commotion. I didn't expect the Order to have a research facility hidden deep underwater.
Sounds like a whole mess to clean up there.
I'll report everything to Ms. Carlotta. With the Common Echoes involved, the families, the Order... No, the entire city will need to unite.
Thank you for bringing this information to us.
If what Fenrico said is true, the Order should make the right choice here.
But that's not something I need to worry about at my pay grade.
If the situation in Septimont is still unclear, you should go there first. Don't worry about us. Ragunna will be fine.
